findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name ZipEntry name used in file construction without traversal check
 * @description ZipEntry.getName() results used in File construction - potential Zip Slip
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/zip-slip-file-construction
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-022
 */

import java

from MethodCall getNameCall, Expr fileArg, ClassInstanceExpr cie
where
  getNameCall.getMethod().hasName("getName") and
  getNameCall.getMethod().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("java.util.zip", "ZipEntry") and
  (
    cie.getConstructor().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("java.io", "File") or
    cie.getConstructor().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("java.io", "FileOutputStream")
  ) and
  fileArg = cie.getAnArgument() and
  (
    // Direct use
    fileArg = getNameCall or
    // Used in concatenation
    getNameCall.getParent*() = fileArg
  )
select cie, "File construction using ZipEntry.getName() at $@ - potential Zip Slip path traversal vulnerability",
  getNameCall, "ZipEntry.getName()"
findings.hypothesis: Zip Slip: ZipEntry.getName() used in file construction

message.text: File construction using ZipEntry.getName() at [ZipEntry.getName()](1) - potential Zip Slip path traversal vulnerability
File construction using ZipEntry.getName() at [ZipEntry.getName()](1) - potential Zip Slip path traversal vulnerability
File construction using ZipEntry.getName() at [ZipEntry.getName()](1) - potential Zip Slip path traversal vulnerability

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name ZipEntry name used in file construction - full context
 * @description Detailed view of ZipEntry.getName() usage in file construction in ItemImport
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/zip-slip-context
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-022
 */

import java

from MethodCall getNameCall, ClassInstanceExpr cie, Method enclosingMethod
where
  getNameCall.getMethod().hasName("getName") and
  getNameCall.getMethod().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("java.util.zip", "ZipEntry") and
  (
    cie.getConstructor().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("java.io", "File") or
    cie.getConstructor().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("java.io", "FileOutputStream")
  ) and
  (
    cie.getAnArgument() = getNameCall or
    getNameCall.getParent*() = cie.getAnArgument()
  ) and
  enclosingMethod = cie.getEnclosingCallable() and
  // Check if there's no path traversal check nearby (no contains("..") check)
  not exists(MethodCall containsCheck |
    containsCheck.getMethod().hasName("contains") and
    containsCheck.getEnclosingCallable() = enclosingMethod and
    exists(StringLiteral sl | sl.getValue().matches("%..%") and sl = containsCheck.getAnArgument())
  ) and
  not exists(MethodCall containsCheck |
    containsCheck.getMethod().hasName("startsWith") and
    containsCheck.getEnclosingCallable() = enclosingMethod
  )
select cie, "Zip Slip: File constructed from ZipEntry.getName() in method '$@' with no path traversal check",
  enclosingMethod, enclosingMethod.getName()
findings.hypothesis: Zip Slip without any path traversal checks in the enclosing method

message.text: Zip Slip: File constructed from ZipEntry.getName() in method '[unzip](1)' with no path traversal check
Zip Slip: File constructed from ZipEntry.getName() in method '[unzip](1)' with no path traversal check
Zip Slip: File constructed from ZipEntry.getName() in method '[unzip](1)' with no path traversal check

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name ControlledVocabulary path from HTTP request
 * @description Check if ControlledVocabulary/ControlledVocabularyServlet uses request parameters in file paths
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/vocab-path-from-request
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-022
 */

import java

from MethodCall vocabCall, MethodCall requestCall, Method m
where
  (
    vocabCall.getMethod().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("org.dspace.vocabulary", "ControlledVocabulary") or
    vocabCall.getMethod().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("org.dspace.content.authority", "DSpaceControlledVocabulary")
  ) and
  (
    requestCall.getMethod().hasName("getParameter") or
    requestCall.getMethod().hasName("getPathInfo") or
    requestCall.getMethod().hasName("getServletPath")
  ) and
  requestCall.getMethod().getDeclaringType().getASupertype*().hasQualifiedName("javax.servlet.http", "HttpServletRequest") and
  m = vocabCall.getEnclosingCallable() and
  requestCall.getEnclosingCallable() = m
select vocabCall, "ControlledVocabulary called in same method as HTTP request parameter ($@) - potential path traversal",
  requestCall, requestCall.getMethod().getName() + "()"
findings.hypothesis: ControlledVocabulary file access triggered by HTTP request parameters

message.text: ControlledVocabulary called in same method as HTTP request parameter ([getParameter()](1)) - potential path traversal

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name JSONControlledVocabularyReader - vocabulary parameter to file access
 * @description Analyze how the vocabulary name from HTTP param flows to file loading
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/json-vocab-reader-detail
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-022
 */

import java

from MethodCall requestCall, MethodCall vocabCall, Method m
where
  m.getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("org.dspace.app.xmlui.cocoon", "JSONControlledVocabularyReader") and
  requestCall.getMethod().hasName("getParameter") and
  requestCall.getEnclosingCallable() = m and
  vocabCall.getEnclosingCallable() = m
select m, "JSONControlledVocabularyReader method '$@' reads request parameter '$@' and calls vocabulary method '$@'",
  m, m.getName(),
  requestCall, requestCall.getMethod().getName() + "(" + requestCall.getArgument(0).toString() + ")",
  vocabCall, vocabCall.getMethod().getDeclaringType().getName() + "." + vocabCall.getMethod().getName()
findings.hypothesis: JSONControlledVocabularyReader passes HTTP param to vocabulary/file loading

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name File construction with string concatenation involving path variable
 * @description Look for File/FileInputStream constructed from concatenated strings with path-like variable names
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity warning
 * @id java/file-concat-path-var
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-022
 */

import java

from ClassInstanceExpr cie, VarAccess va, Variable v
where
  (
    cie.getConstructor().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("java.io", "File") or
    cie.getConstructor().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("java.io", "FileInputStream")
  ) and
  va.getVariable() = v and
  (
    v.getName().toLowerCase().matches("%path%") or
    v.getName().toLowerCase().matches("%file%") or
    v.getName().toLowerCase().matches("%theme%") or
    v.getName().toLowerCase().matches("%resource%")
  ) and
  va.getParent*() = cie.getAnArgument() and
  cie.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().getPackage().getName().matches("org.dspace.app.xmlui%")
select cie, "File construction using variable '$@' in xmlui component - verify path is validated against base directory",
  va, v.getName()
findings.hypothesis: File construction using path-like variables in xmlui resource serving components

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name Unsafe path construction in ControlledVocabulary
 * @description ControlledVocabulary may load files from user-supplied paths
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity warning
 * @id java/controlled-vocabulary-path
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-022
 */

import java

from ClassInstanceExpr cie, Method m
where
  (
    cie.getConstructor().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("java.io", "File") or
    cie.getConstructor().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("java.io", "FileInputStream")
  ) and
  m = cie.getEnclosingCallable() and
  (
    m.getDeclaringType().getName().matches("%Vocabulary%") or
    m.getDeclaringType().getName().matches("%News%") or
    m.getDeclaringType().getName().matches("%Theme%")
  )
select cie, "File construction in $@ - verify the path cannot be controlled by user input",
  m, m.getDeclaringType().getName() + "." + m.getName()
findings.hypothesis: File construction in vocabulary/news/theme serving classes

message.text: File construction in [DSpaceControlledVocabulary.initPluginNames](1) - verify the path cannot be controlled by user input
